content.problemStatement:
There are NNN space stations orbiting around the Earth, and CiC_iCi‚Äã indicates the maximum capacity the ithi^{th}ith station can hold.

Additionally, there are QQQ missions that must be carried out. Each mission has two requirements: a crew requirement XXX and a storage requirement YYY.

The crew must all stay in a single cabin, while the other N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 cabins can be used for storage.

If cabin iii is chosen for the crew to stay in, the following conditions must be satisfied:


X‚â§CiX \leq C_iX‚â§Ci‚Äã, so that there's enough space for the entire crew.
YYY should not exceed the sum of the other N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 elements of CCC, i.e. C1+C2+‚Ä¶+Ci‚àí1+Ci+1+‚Ä¶+CNC_1 + C_2 + \ldots + C_{i-1} + C_{i+1} + \ldots + C_NC1‚Äã+C2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+Ci‚àí1‚Äã+Ci+1‚Äã+‚Ä¶+CN‚Äã, to satisfy the storage condition.
It's possible that no valid choice of cabin exists. To meet the demands of a mission, we can expand the capacity of any space station by spending 111 token per additional unit of capacity.

That is, using one token, you can increase any single CiC_iCi‚Äã by 111.

For each mission, determine the minimum number of tokens needed to ensure that there exists a choice of crew cabin which satisfies both conditions. Under the constraints, it can be proven to be always possible.

Note that each mission is to be processed independently, considering the initial capacities CCC.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases.
Each test case contains of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains one integer NNN ‚Äî the number of space stations.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äã ‚Äî the space station capacities.
The third line contains one integer QQQ ‚Äî the number of missions.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers XiX_iXi‚Äã and YiY_iYi‚Äã, denoting the parameters of the iii-th mission.




Output Format
For each test case, output QQQ space-separated integers, where the ithi^{th}ith integer represents the minimum tokens needed for the ithi^{th}ith mission.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 :

Mission 111: Crew: 555 and Storage: 777 - We can fit the crew in Station 333, and the rest of the stations have sufficient space for storage needs.
Mission 222: Crew: 777 and Storage: 999 - We can fit the crew in Station 333, and upgrade any 1 from the remaining stations to get sufficient space for storage needs.
